paper_title: 3D Digitization and Phylogenetic Analysis of Albicetus

methods.description: Process for collecting and rendering three-dimensional datasets of the surface topology of the specimen using structured light scanning and CT scanning, to facilitate morphological comparisons and visualization.

steps.description: Collected three-dimensional datasets of its surface topology using an Artec Eva structured light scanner, scanning at 8 frames per second.
steps.searchable_summary: collect 3D surface topology data using Artec Eva scanner

steps.description: Compiled datasets in Geomagic version 2012 and Meshlab to render a 3D surface model of the type specimen.
steps.searchable_summary: compile and render 3D model using Geomagic and Meshlab

steps.description: CT scanned a small isolated tooth fragment exposing part of the enamel cap in a Nikon Metrology 225kV microfocus CT scanning system.
steps.searchable_summary: CT scan tooth fragment using Nikon Metrology system

steps.description: 3D models are available for manipulation, visualization, sharing and download through the public data repository Zenodo and the Smithsonian X 3D website.
steps.searchable_summary: make 3D models available on Zenodo and Smithsonian X 3D

methods.description: Procedure to assess the phylogenetic relationships of Albicetus using a matrix of morphological characters and performing cladistic analysis.

steps.description: Edited a matrix of 42 morphological characters, adding one additional character to code for the mesorostral groove.
steps.searchable_summary: edit morphological character matrix to include mesorostral groove

steps.description: Performed a heuristic search using the tree bisection-reconnection (TBR) algorithm on PAUP.
steps.searchable_summary: perform heuristic search using TBR algorithm in PAUP

steps.description: Conducted statistical support analyses by searching for successively longer trees to calculate decay indices and 100 bootstrap replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate decay indices and perform bootstrap replicates for statistical support
